step_by_step_thinking:
Option B states "All stains are highly susceptible to penicillin." According to various articles discussing N. gonorrhoeae's susceptibility, there is increasing resistance to penicillins, especially in certain regions where multidrug-resistant strains exist. Thus, option B is incorrect because not all stains remain fully susceptible to penicillin anymore given current resistant trends.
\boxed{B} Based on the review of the literature, Option D stating "Found exclusively in human beings" aligns with historical and biological knowledge about Neisseria gonorrhoeae being strictly host-specific. No contradicting evidence exists suggesting otherwise.
